cases.passage: A researcher is studying the properties of coordination compounds in various industrial applications. The researcher comes across a compound with the formula [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl, which is used as a catalyst in the production of polyethylene. The compound is found to be stable in air but decomposes when heated to high temperatures. The researcher is interested in understanding the structure and bonding of this compound.

questions.q: What is the oxidation state of the cobalt ion in the compound [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl?
questions.explanation: The oxidation state of the cobalt ion can be determined by considering the charges on the ligands and the overall charge on the complex. In this case, the ammonia ligands are neutral, and the chloride ligands have a -1 charge. Since the overall charge on the complex is +1, the oxidation state of the cobalt ion must be +3.

questions.q: What type of isomerism is possible for the compound [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl?
questions.explanation: The compound [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl has a coordination number of 6, which means it has an octahedral geometry. Since the chloride ligands are in a cis configuration, the compound can exhibit geometrical isomerism.

questions.q: What is the name of the compound [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl?
questions.explanation: The name of the compound is determined by the number and type of ligands attached to the central metal ion. In this case, the compound has four ammonia ligands and two chloride ligands, and the overall charge is +1.

questions.q: What is the coordination number of the cobalt ion in the compound [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl?
questions.explanation: The coordination number of the cobalt ion is determined by the number of ligands attached to it. In this case, the cobalt ion is attached to four ammonia ligands and two chloride ligands, making the coordination number 6.

questions.q: What is the magnetic property of the compound [Co(NH3)4Cl2]Cl?
questions.explanation: The magnetic property of the compound is determined by the number of unpaired electrons on the central metal ion. In this case, the cobalt ion has four unpaired electrons, making the compound paramagnetic.

cases.passage: A pharmaceutical company is developing a new drug that uses a coordination compound as the active ingredient. The compound has the formula [Pt(NH3)2Cl2] and is used to treat certain types of cancer. The company is interested in understanding the chemical properties of this compound, including its stability and reactivity.

questions.q: What is the oxidation state of the platinum ion in the compound [Pt(NH3)2Cl2]?
questions.explanation: The oxidation state of the platinum ion can be determined by considering the charges on the ligands and the overall charge on the complex. In this case, the ammonia ligands are neutral, and the chloride ligands have a -1 charge. Since the overall charge on the complex is neutral, the oxidation state of the platinum ion must be +2.

questions.q: What type of isomerism is possible for the compound [Pt(NH3)2Cl2]?
questions.explanation: The compound [Pt(NH3)2Cl2] has a coordination number of 4, which means it has a square planar geometry. Since the ammonia ligands are in a cis configuration, the compound can exhibit geometrical isomerism.

questions.q: What is the name of the compound [Pt(NH3)2Cl2]?
questions.explanation: The name of the compound is determined by the number and type of ligands attached to the central metal ion. In this case, the compound has two ammonia ligands and two chloride ligands, and the overall charge is neutral.

questions.q: What is the coordination number of the platinum ion in the compound [Pt(NH3)2Cl2]?
questions.explanation: The coordination number of the platinum ion is determined by the number of ligands attached to it. In this case, the platinum ion is attached to two ammonia ligands and two chloride ligands, making the coordination number 4.

questions.q: What is the magnetic property of the compound [Pt(NH3)2Cl2]?
questions.explanation: The magnetic property of the compound is determined by the number of unpaired electrons on the central metal ion. In this case, the platinum ion has no unpaired electrons, making the compound diamagnetic.
